title: /kernel
summary_text: Yes. The `/kernel` prompt contract explicitly requires writing a bootstrap receipt **before first acknowledgement**, and separately says `BLOCKED` is valid when neither normal nor degraded shape can be established ([kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md):61-66,97-99). Current implementation gap: `codex-kernel-guard launch` validates shape first and can `die` on missing `glm`/specialist before receipt write ([codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh):524-532,484-487). So blocked startup can end up with no receipt. Least misleading local action when external provider evidence is unavailable: 1. Write a **truthful** bootstrap receipt anyway (actual providers/models; do not claim specialist/GLM evidence). 2. Immediately compute health and report `BLOCKED` (never print “Kernel orchestration is active…”). Minimal command shape: ```bash KERNEL_RUN_ID=<run_id> \ KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_ACTIVE_MODELS_CSV=<actual_models_csv> \ KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_MANIFEST_LANE_COUNT=<actual_manifest_lane_count> \ KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_AGENT_LABELS=true \ KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_SUBAGENT_LABELS=true \ bash scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh write <lane_count> <providers_csv> normal "blocked: provider evidence unavailable" KERNEL_RUN_ID=<run_id> bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh status-json ``` This is consistent with health semantics (`invalid` -> lifecycle `blocked`) in [kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh):178-239.
